content: Stop hooks guard mulch learn with three checks: (1) command -v mulch exists, (2) .mulch/mulch.config.yaml exists in cwd, (3) not inside a worktree or agent session

name: OverClaw Gateway Architecture
description: Gateway provides REST API for agent management, mail, approval flow, and dashboard UI on separate port 5050
ENOENT mulch.config.yaml when mulch learn runs in agent worktrees that lack .mulch directory
resolution: Guard the stop hook with three checks: command -v mulch, test -f .mulch/mulch.config.yaml, and OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME / worktree path detection

title: Use mulch for agent expertise persistence
rationale: Agents wake up fresh each session; mulch provides structured, queryable expertise records that survive across sessions and can be scoped per domain

name: mulch-cli
description: Mulch CLI v0.4.2 installed via bun (bun install -g mulch-cli). Provides structured expertise records for coding agents.

content: Three.js depth sorting with renderOrder: Sort by renderOrder first (lower=background), then by depth position. Use sortByDepthAndRenderOrder function that checks renderOrder via nullish coalescing (a.renderOrder ?? 0).

name: threejs-vitest-testing
description: Farm sim visualizer testing: Use Vitest with jsdom environment for Three.js applications. Mock global.camera, global.worldGroup, and DOM elements (document.getElementById) before each test. Test async init methods, dispose cleanup, and DOM interaction with null checks.

name: configurable-asset-paths
description: Asset path configuration in Vite apps: Use a configurable ASSET_BASE with window.FARM_SIM_ASSET_BASE fallback to enable flexible asset path configuration without code changes. Useful for development vs production asset locations.

resolution: Always clear existing timer (setInterval return = null) before creating new one. Store timer ID and clear in dispose/close methods.

name: Agent Garden Creation Tasks
description: List the 10 specific tasks needed for agents to create their gardens (worktree workspaces): 1) Git worktree creation with isolated branch, 2) Agent overlay generation (.claude/CLAUDE.md) with identity/context/scope, 3) Task specification creation (.overstory/specs/<task-id>.md), 4) Bead issue creation in .beads/issues.jsonl, 5) tmux session setup with environment, 6) Mailbox initialization for communication, 7) Environment variable configuration (OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME, NANOBOT_*), 8) Agent manifest registration, 9) Mulch expertise setup (optional), 10) Hooks configuration. See .claude/agent-garden-creation-tasks.md for complete documentation.

content: Agent task IDs use two formats: workspace-<short-hash> (bead-based) and oc-<uuid-8chars> (gateway-generated). Documentation should reference both.

name: bead-and-spec-atomic-creation
description: The _create_bead_and_spec function in gateway (line 169-247) creates both the spec file and bead entry atomically before agent spawn.

title: Mandatory reviewer before merge
rationale: Reviewer PASS is required before sending merge_ready to prevent bugs that cost 10-50x more to fix after merge. Skipping review is a false economy — reviewers cost ~2000 tokens but catch integration issues and edge cases.

name: orphaned-lead-coordination
description: When injected as lead for orphaned workers: (1) check overstory status for active sessions, (2) check mail list --all for pending approvals, (3) if workers already sent worker_done and merge_ready was sent, issue is merge queue drain. Send result mail to coordinator with branch list requesting overstory merge --all. Lead cannot merge manually - violates PATH_BOUNDARY_VIOLATION.
Orphaned worktrees accumulate when previous swarm session ends before merge drain processes completed branches
resolution: Coordinator merge drain should run on session start or use webhook trigger. Lead agents report orphaned branches to coordinator via mail with --type result

name: farm-sim-threejs-architecture
description: Farm sim visualizer uses Three.js r183+ with Vite 6, sprite sheets from Tiny Wonder Farm/PIPOYA assets. Structure: Agent class (movement, thoughts, dialogue), Garden class (crops, soil), Mailbox class (flag, mail), dialogueSystem (typewriter effect). Key issues: memory leaks (textureCache, setInterval), no DOM null checks, asset path hardcoding, missing tests.

resolution: Builder completed without tests, had memory leaks (textureCache never cleared, setInterval stacking), DOM access without null checks, hardcoded asset paths. Must enforce: test requirements, memory cleanup patterns, asset path configurability, DOM existence checks.

name: threejs-memory-cleanup
description: Three.js memory cleanup pattern: Always implement dispose() methods that clear textureCache Map, call texture.dispose() on all textures, and clear setInterval timers. Use cleanupTextureCache() and releaseTexture(url) functions for proper cleanup. Essential for long-running WebGL apps.

name: vitest-dom-mocking
description: Vitest DOM mocking pattern: Mock DOM elements with vi.fn() for classList methods (add, remove) to verify UI updates without browser environment. Use beforeEach to reset mocks and create element references.

name: 2d-depth-sorting
description: 2.5D depth sorting: Use renderOrder for explicit layering first, then depth-based sorting for same-layer sprites. Formula: depth = position.y + position.z * 0.01. Sort groups by renderOrder ascending, then by depth ascending.

name: threejs-farm-sim-sprite-sheets
description: Farm sim visualizer with Three.js sprite sheets: Use createGridSprite for uniform sprite sheets and createRegionSprite for irregular packed objects. Set texture.magFilter = THREE.NearestFilter and texture.minFilter = THREE.NearestFilter for crisp pixel art. Agent thought bubbles use HTML overlay positioned via screenPosition.project(camera) with italic text styling.

name: traditional-rpg-dialogue-ui
description: Traditional RPG dialogue boxes: Use HTML overlay with CSS animations (dialogueIn keyframe). Include portrait canvas, agent name, typewriter text effect (setInterval character-by-character), and blinking 'Press SPACE or Click to continue' hint. Close with Escape or click. Position absolutely bottom-center with gradient background (f5e6c8 to e8d4a8) and border-radius.

name: vitest-jsdom-testing-setup
description: Vitest with jsdom for testing browser JS: vitest.config.js with environment: 'jsdom', globals: true. Mock THREE.js with vi.mock() including all constructors (Scene, Camera, Renderer, Clock, etc.). Mock DOM with document.getElementById returning predefined objects. Test classes by defining inline test versions or exporting from main.js via global object.

name: memory-leak-prevention-js
description: Memory leak prevention patterns: (1) Track cloned textures in Set() and dispose in cleanup. (2) Clear setInterval timers before creating new ones, store timer reference and nullify after clearInterval. (3) Use beforeunload event for cleanup. (4) Dispose all THREE.js materials/geometries/textures. (5) Clear Maps/Sets holding object references.
